ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: A Korean-style ragù—ground pork simmered in an aromatic, spiced sauce tossed with soft rice cakes and tender baby bok choy.
items: 10 oz ground pork
½ lb Korean rice cakes (tteok)
4 oz baby bok choy
1 bunch garlic chives
1 yellow onion
1 inch piece fresh ginger
2 tbsp black bean sauce
2 tbsp crème fraîche
1 tbsp gochujang
1 tbsp soy glaze
2 tsp oil
Salt and pepper to taste
ingredients.callout: Gochujang note: Start with ½ tbsp and add more to taste—this Korean red chili paste brings the heat. Adjust to your spice preference.

body.html: A Korean-style ragù—ground pork cooked down in an aromatic, spiced sauce with ginger, onion, and garlic chives, then tossed with soft rice cakes (tteok) and tender baby bok choy. Finished with a touch of crème fraîche and soy glaze.

items: Prep — wash, chop, and mince aromatics (10 min)
Cook the pork — brown ground pork on medium heat
Build flavor — add ginger, onion, garlic chives, bok choy
Finish — simmer rice cakes in the sauce until coated (20–30 min total)

body.html: Pro tip: Break up the pork as it cooks to get small, tender pieces that coat evenly with sauce.

items: Heat a medium pot of salted water to boiling on high (for the rice cakes later).
Wash and dry all fresh produce.
Peel and mince the ginger into small pieces.
Thinly slice the garlic chives.
Peel and small dice the yellow onion.
Roughly chop the baby bok choy, separating stems from leaves.
Heat a large pan (nonstick preferred) over medium heat.
Add 2 tsp oil. Once hot, add the ground pork.
Season with salt and pepper. Cook for 4–6 minutes, frequently breaking the meat apart with a spoon.
Cook until browned and cooked through.
To the pan of cooked pork, add the minced ginger, diced onion, and half of the garlic chives.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for 2–3 minutes until softened and fragrant.

slides.title: Finish the Ragù
slides.checkboxLabel: Sauce thickened and coating the pork

items: To the pan, add the bok choy, black bean sauce, crème fraîche, soy glaze, and ½ cup water.
Add ½ tbsp gochujang to start (you can add more for extra spice).
Stir to combine. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 4–6 minutes until the sauce has thickened and coats the pork.

body.html: Spice control: Taste as you go. Add more gochujang ½ tbsp at a time if you like more heat.

items: While the ragù simmers, add the rice cakes to the pot of boiling water.
Cook for 2–3 minutes, or until tender. Drain thoroughly.
Transfer the cooked rice cakes to the pan of ragù.
Season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 2–3 minutes until the rice cakes are thoroughly coated.
Remove from heat and season again to taste.
Divide the finished ragù and rice cakes between 2 bowls or dishes.
Garnish with the remaining garlic chives.
Enjoy!
Optional toppings: sesame seeds, a fried egg, extra gochujang, kimchi, or toasted nori
